icp_classification.classification_rationale: Series B closed Jan 2026 ($18M) + job post velocity +240% in 60 days + AI maturity score 2 (emerging capability). Strong Segment 1 fit with Segment 4 overlay for ML platform engineering.

funding_event.brief_language: NexusAI Labs closed an $18M Series B on January 14, 2026 — 98 days ago. Fresh budget, likely in active team-building mode.
funding_event.pitch_implication: Budget is confirmed and runway clock is running. Frame as time-sensitive capacity support.

job_post_velocity.source: Wellfound public job feed (scraped via Playwright, April 2026 snapshot)
job_post_velocity.brief_language: 12 open engineering roles, up from 5 sixty days ago (+140%). More than half are AI-adjacent (ML Eng, LLM Infra, Data Platform). Hiring velocity is outpacing what a 47-person company can recruit in-house.
job_post_velocity.pitch_implication: Classic Segment 1 buying trigger. In-house recruiting cannot keep up with post-Series B headcount demands.

layoff_event.brief_language: No layoff events in the last 120 days. Not a Segment 2 (restructuring) signal.
layoff_event.pitch_implication: Absence of layoff signal confirms this is a growth play, not a cost-cutting engagement.

leadership_change.brief_language: No leadership change detected. Founding CTO in place. Not a Segment 3 (leadership transition) trigger.
leadership_change.pitch_implication: No narrow vendor-reassessment window. Focus on Segment 1 and Segment 4 angles.

tech_stack.source: BuiltWith public data + GitHub org activity

details.github_recent_activity: Active — 23 commits in last 30 days on inference-optimization and model-training repos

tech_stack.brief_language: Python/FastAPI/K8s/Ray stack. Strong bench match: Tenacious has available Python data engineers, MLOps engineers, and K8s platform engineers on bench.
tech_stack.pitch_implication: Bench-to-brief match is confirmed. Can commit to Python/MLOps capacity pending bench availability check.

ai_maturity.source: Composite: job posts, GitHub org, press, team page
ai_maturity.score_description: Active AI work in progress. Multiple AI-adjacent roles open. GitHub activity on AI/ML repos. Early executive commitment to AI as product core. Has not yet established a named Head of AI or VP Data.

per_signal_breakdown.evidence: 4 of 7 engineering openings are AI-adjacent (ML Eng, LLM Infra, Data Platform, Applied Science)
No Head of AI or VP Data on public team page. CTO manages AI direction.
23 commits in 30 days on inference-optimization repo. Public ML tooling work.
CTO referenced 'building the AI-native platform stack' in Feb 2026 TechCrunch interview
dbt, Snowflake, Ray detected via BuiltWith and job descriptions

ai_maturity.confidence_note: Score of 2 inferred from a mix of high-weight (roles) and medium-weight (GitHub, exec commentary) inputs. No single definitive indicator. Language should ask rather than assert on AI leadership maturity.

pitch_language_guidance.segment_1_language: Scale your AI team faster than in-house hiring can support — you're clearly building AI-native infrastructure and the recruiting bottleneck for ML engineers is real at this stage.
pitch_language_guidance.segment_4_language: Your infrastructure signals active ML platform work — Tenacious has engineers who've built similar inference optimization layers at Series B and C stage companies.

bench_to_brief_check.commitment_note: Bench availability confirmed from bench summary (updated 2026-04-21). Safe to reference 2-3 engineer availability. DO NOT commit to specific headcount without delivery lead sign-off.

outreach_brief_summary: NexusAI Labs closed an $18M Series B 98 days ago and has nearly tripled open engineering roles since. With 4 of 7 openings AI-adjacent and active GitHub commits on inference optimization, they're building AI-native infrastructure faster than a 47-person team can recruit. The typical bottleneck at this stage isn't budget — it's finding ML engineers who can ship production-quality systems. Tenacious has Python/MLOps engineers available with exactly this background.

confidence_summary.lowest_confidence_signal: ai_maturity (0.78 — medium confidence, should ask rather than assert)
